definition: A CD1c-positive myeloid dendritic cell that constitutes an inflammatory subset within the conventional dendritic cell type 2 (cDC2) compartment, characterized in humans by a CD5-negative, CD163-positive, CD14-positive surface phenotype and expression of inflammasome-related genes, EREG, FCN1, VCAN, and S100A8/S100A9 (PMID:31491384, PMID:32640257). This cell develops via a GM-CSF-dependent pathway independent of both cDC-restricted progenitors (CDP) and monocyte-restricted progenitors (cMoP), and is FLT3L-responsive (PMID:32640257). It is capable of activating naive T cells and displays a distinctive ability to prime CD8-positive, CD103-positive tissue-resident memory T cells through TGF-beta signaling (PMID:32640257). In human skin, this cell type produces IL1B and IL23A upon activation (PMID:34129014).

logical_axioms.notes: DC3 are CD5-negative within the cDC2 compartment, distinguishing them from DC2
CD163 expression distinguishes DC3 from DC2 within the cDC2 compartment
CD14 expression is a key marker of the most inflammatory DC3 subset

references.supports: Identification and characterization of DC3 as a distinct CD5-CD163+CD14+ inflammatory dendritic cell subset within the cDC2 compartment; defining surface markers and expansion in SLE.
DC3 ontogeny via GM-CSF-dependent pathway independent of CDP and cMoP; FLT3L responsiveness; capacity to prime CD8+CD103+ tissue-resident memory T cells.
Original identification of DC2 and DC3 subpopulations within CD1c+ dendritic cells using single-cell RNA-seq; inflammasome gene expression in DC3.
Identification of CD14+ DC3 in human skin tissue; co-production of IL1B and IL23A in psoriasis; enrichment of DC3 in inflammatory skin.

justification: The CL description of CL:0002399 (CD1c-positive myeloid dendritic cell) explicitly acknowledges that cDC2 'can be further subclassified into DC2 and DC3 cells' but no dedicated DC3 term exists. DC3/inflammatory DCs have been characterized as a distinct lineage with unique ontogeny (GM-CSF-dependent, CDP/cMoP-independent), distinct surface markers (CD5-, CD163+, CD14+), unique functional properties (IL1B/IL23A co-production, CD8+CD103+ T cell priming), and pathological relevance (expanded in SLE, enriched in psoriatic skin). Multiple single-cell atlas studies now annotate DC3/inflammatory DC as a separate cell state, including the Gopee et al. (2024) prenatal skin atlas where it appears in fetal skin. A dedicated CL term is needed to enable precise annotation of this increasingly recognized cell type.
ntr_markdown:
## Preferred term label
type 3 dendritic cell

## Synonyms
- DC3 (exact)
- inflammatory dendritic cell (exact)
- CD14-positive type 3 dendritic cell (narrow)
- CD163-positive CD14-positive DC3 (narrow)

## Definition
A CD1c-positive myeloid dendritic cell that constitutes an inflammatory subset within the conventional dendritic cell type 2 (cDC2) compartment, characterized in humans by a CD5-negative, CD163-positive, CD14-positive surface phenotype and expression of inflammasome-related genes, EREG, FCN1, VCAN, and S100A8/S100A9 (PMID:31491384, PMID:32640257). This cell develops via a GM-CSF-dependent pathway independent of both cDC-restricted progenitors (CDP) and monocyte-restricted progenitors (cMoP), and is FLT3L-responsive (PMID:32640257). It is capable of activating naive T cells and displays a distinctive ability to prime CD8-positive, CD103-positive tissue-resident memory T cells through TGF-beta signaling (PMID:32640257). In human skin, this cell type produces IL1B and IL23A upon activation (PMID:34129014).

## Parent cell type term
CL:0002399 CD1c-positive myeloid dendritic cell

## Anatomical structure where the cell type is found
UBERON:0002097 skin of body (also found in blood, lymph nodes, and other tissues)

## Logical axioms
- SubClassOf: CD1c-positive myeloid dendritic cell (CL:0002399)
- SubClassOf: lacks_plasma_membrane_part some CD5 (PR:000001839)
- SubClassOf: has_plasma_membrane_part some CD163 (PR:000001501)
- SubClassOf: has_plasma_membrane_part some CD14 (PR:000001889)

## Key references
- Dutertre et al. (2019) Immunity. DOI:10.1016/j.immuni.2019.08.008 PMID:31491384
- Bourdely et al. (2020) Immunity. DOI:10.1016/j.immuni.2020.06.002 PMID:32640257
- Villani et al. (2017) Science. DOI:10.1126/science.aah4573 PMID:28428369
- Nakamizo et al. (2021) J. Exp. Med. DOI:10.1084/jem.20202345 PMID:34129014

## Justification
The CL currently lacks a dedicated term for DC3/type 3 dendritic cells, despite their recognition as a functionally distinct inflammatory DC subset with unique ontogeny. The existing CL:0002399 definition acknowledges DC3 exist but does not provide a separate term. DC3 have been identified in multiple single-cell atlases and are increasingly annotated as distinct cell states in tissue studies, including the prenatal human skin atlas (Gopee et al. 2024, Nature).
